item.title: 5 reasons Linux is the best OS for coding
item.itunes:title: 5 reasons Linux is the best OS for coding
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Programming, unlike gaming and video editing, doesn’t require a top-of-the-line PC rocking the latest processor and graphics card. The minimal hardware requirement makes it easy to get into coding even if you’re on a Chromebook.

However, the choice of your operating system can have a considerable impact on your coding journey. Although Linux continues to lag behind Windows 11 when it comes to gaming and graphics designing, there are plenty of reasons to pick a Linux distribution if you’re into programming.

1 Linux is open-source, community-driven, and highly customizable

Thanks to the GNU General Public License, the entire source codes of the Linux kernel and the Linux distributions are open-source and fully transparent, meaning you can modify the UI of your favorite Linux distro by launching a code editor and tweaking its source code. Linux is also very community-driven and well documented, making it easy to troubleshoot any issues you may encounter when deploying your apps. Unlike Windows 11's buggy updates, you won't have to fix bugs or deal with compatibility issues in your apps every time a new update is released for your favorite Linux distro.

Additionally, Linux distros provide limitless customizability as you can handpick the apps, modules, and services you want for your system. Linux is more POSIX compliant than Windows, too. So, any apps you create on Linux can be easily ported to macOS and other UNIX-based operating systems.

2 Linux has minimal system requirements

Acer Swift 3 (2022)

As I mentioned earlier, you don’t need a powerful PC to create, edit, and debug programs. If you have a decade-old system lying around, you can breathe some new life into it by installing Linux. You can even ditch the desktop environment for a headless SSH setup if you have a dinosaur rig from the early 2000s.

In contrast, Windows 11 requires you to have at least 4GB of RAM, 64GB of storage, and a 64-bit CPU that supports TPM version 2. In addition to their lesser memory consumption, Linux distributions are (mostly) free from bloatware and ads that come bundled with Microsoft’s latest OS. Not to mention, most software development tools were primarily designed for UNIX-based systems before they were ported to Windows, so you’ll get better performance out of most IDEs and code editors on Linux.

3 Linux has better package managers

Suppose you need to install Python on your Windows system. First, you’ll have to navigate to the official website to download the latest version of Python. Then, you’ll need to execute the .exe file and specify the directory before you can set up Python. If you want another Integrated Development Environment (IDE), you’ll have to follow the same process as above. And that’s before we consider the other libraries you may need to download for your project. This is where package managers come in handy; you can install all your libraries, modules, and packages just by entering a line into the OS terminal.

Although Windows supports a couple of package managers like Winget and Chocolatey, they can’t compete with APT, Pacman, DPKG, RPM, and other package managers included by default on Linux. For one, Windows package managers lack the sheer number of modules offered by their Linux counterparts. Even though Chocolatey is one of the best apps for installing packages on Windows, it requires a paid subscription if you wish to use all its premium features. Compare that with the free-to-use Linux package managers that support tons of modules on top of being more integrated with your Linux distro, and it's easy to see why Linux takes the cake when it comes to the simplicity of sharing and downloading packages.

4 Learning Linux makes server management a lot easier

Source: Lenovo

With its fast performance, robust security features, and high scalability, Linux is the OS of choice for most web servers and cloud platforms. Moreover, most server admin roles require you to be familiar with bash and shell scripting, which are accessible via the powerful Terminals on most Linux distributions.

Since the overall file system, libraries, and terminal commands are largely similar between Linux distros and web servers, getting proficient with the Linux interface will give you an edge when managing a server. Learning the ins and outs of Linux will make it infinitely easier to jump into containerization as most docker and virtual machines are based on Linux.

5 WSL2 can't replace native Linux

Released in 2016, the Windows Subsystem for Linux was Microsoft’s (mostly successful) attempt at making Linux command-line tools and apps compatible with Windows. WSL1 uses a translation layer to run a full-fledged Linux distro on your Windows 11 machine without the performance overhead of a virtual machine, though it doesn't support all the Linux binaries and system calls. Microsoft remedied the issue by switching to a “lightweight utility virtual machine” for the second iteration of WSL. Thanks to its support for the Linux kernel, WSL2 quickly became a solid workaround for anyone looking for a Linux-like development environment on Windows.

That said, WSL2 is still not good enough to completely replace Linux. While WSL2 has a better overall performance than its predecessor, it’s known for hogging a lot of memory. WSL's read and write times also take a hit if you try to modify or save documents to the Windows file system. Finally, the second version of the Windows Subsystem for Linux is still incompatible with serial ports and the OpenCL API.

Should developers ditch Windows and switch to Linux?

No! For the average programmer who isn’t ready to dual-boot Linux and Windows, the latter remains a decent OS. If you're learning the .Net framework and its associated languages, it's better to use Windows as Microsoft's proprietary software framework was designed for the OS. Game development and 3D rendering are other fields where Windows has a leg up on Linux.

But if you want the maximum convenience and efficient resource management, you’d want to go with Linux. In fact, Linux is significantly better for DevOps, server management, and web development. Setting up Linux on your main PC will make it easy to navigate the production environment on top of exposing you to Nagios, Webmin, and other Linux-exclusive development tools.

item.title: Best HP laptops in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best HP laptops in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Few brands in the laptop world are as renowned and popular as HP. From budget-friendly laptops to high-end premium experiences, the company offers some of the best laptops you can buy. If you're looking to upgrade your current personal or work computer, or you want to gift someone else, going with HP is definitely a smart move. But there are plenty of options to choose from in the company's lineup and choosing the right one for you may prove difficult. To help you with that, we've rounded up the best HP laptops you can buy today.

HP covers a lot of ground, and whether you want a Windows 11 laptop or a Chromebook, there's something for you. We've chosen models geared at all kinds of different users, so whatever you're looking for, we've got you covered.

  • HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
    Best overall
    $900 at Best Buy
  • Omen 17 (Intel, 2023)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1556 at Amazon
  • HP Dragonfly G4
    Best business laptop
    $1401 at HP
  • HP Spectre x360 16 (2023)
    Best 15/16-inch laptop
    $1200 at HP
  • HP Envy 17 (2023)
    Best 17-inch laptop
    $650 at HP
  • HP Envy 16 (2023)
    Best laptop for creators
    $1199 at B&H
  • HP Pavilion Aero 13 (2023)
    Best affordable lightweight laptop
    $580 at HP
  • HP Dragonfly Pro Chromebook
    Best Chromebook
    $1000 at HP
  • HP Chromebook Plus 15a
    Best affordable Chromebook
    $349 at Best Buy

Our top picks for HP laptops in 2023

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best overall

A premium laptop with very few flaws

$900 $1500 Save $600

The 2023 HP Spectre x360 13.5 packs 13th-generation Intel Core processors in a sleek and stylish design. It also has a great 13.5-inch display with a 3:2 aspect ratio that makes it optimal for productivity.

Pros
  • Great performance and battery life with 13th-gen Intel processors
  • Premium all-aluminum design with a dual-tone look
  • Tall 3:2 display is great for working on the go
  • 5MP webcam
Cons
  • 5G support not yet available for this year's model
$950 at HP $900 at Best Buy $1350 at Best Buy (OLED)

The HP Spectre x360 is one of the most iconic Windows laptops out there, and with the new Spectre x360 13.5, it's just as easy to recommend as previous models.

Performanmce-wise, the HP Spectre x360 13.5 is packing up to a 13th-generation Intel Core i7-1355U, with 10 cores and 12 threads delivering strong performance and great battery life for a Windows laptop. You also get 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD for storage, though more configurations are likely to show up over time.

The 13.5-inch display on the HP Spectre x360 is also great, featuring a 3:2 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution in the base model. You have the option to get a 3K2K OLED panel, too, which is even sharper and produces extremely vibrant colors and pure blacks, making for a fantastic viewing experience on top of being a great canvas for work. Above the display is a 5MP webcam, which continues to be one of the best you can find on any laptop, and it also supports Windows Hello. It has some smart features, too, like auto framing and lighting correction.

HP hasn't really changed the design for this year, so we get the typical dual-tone look in both Nightfall Black and Nocturne Blue colorways.It looks great, while still being somewhat subdued. Being an all-aluminum laptop, the SPectre x360 13.5 weighs in at 3.01 pounds. A single-tone Natural Silver model should also be available at some point. As for ports, we're looking at two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader, a solid setup for a laptop of this size.

All in all, this is a fantastic premium convertible that gives you a tall high-quality screen, a striking premium design, and great performance all around. It's definitely one of the best laptops HP makes.

Omen 17 (Intel, 2023)
Best gaming laptop

Powerful performance and a smooth display

$1556 $1700 Save $144

The Omen 17 is HP's most powerful gaming laptop, with 13th-generation Intel HX-series processors and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU delivering outstanding performance. You also get a great display for gaming and up to 32GB of RAM.

Pros
  • Top-tier performance with 13th-gen Intel CPUs and Nvidia RTX 40 series GPUs
  • Sleek design with RGB for some flair
  • Plenty of ports
Cons
  • Battery life is abysmal
  • Still using a 720p webcam without Windows Hello
$1700 at HP (Core i7) $2600 at HP (Core i9) $1556 at Amazon

HP also caters to gamers with its Omen lineup of laptops, and the Omen 17 is the cream of the crop if you want to get into gaming.

Performance is the name of the game here, and with up to an Intel Core i9-13900HX with 24 cores and 32 threads, the Omen 17 is off to a very good start. What's more, that's paired with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, giving you enough power to run just about any modern game with no issues. You also get up 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

The display is a 17.3-inch panel and it comes in the typical 16:9 aspect ratio, which is becoming less common. TYThe base model is Full HD and has a 144Hz refresh rate, but you can upgrade to a Quad HD panel and choose either 165Hz or 240Hz models depending on how smooth you want your games to be. Both options are very good, though. The same can't be said for the webcam, which is still a 720p camera with no Windows Hello support.

In terms of design, the Omen 17 looks fairly sleek and clean, and even though it does have RGB, it's done in a very subdued way so it's not obnoxious at all. It's still a heavy laptop, though, starting at 6.1 pounds, and that's to be expected with such powerful specs. You also get a healthy supply of ports. There are three USB Type-A ports (which many peripherals still use), a Thunderbolt 4/USB-C port, HDMI 2.1, mini DisplayPort, Gigabit Ethernet, microSD card reader, and a headphone jack. Plus, of course, a proprietary power connector.

Overall, the Omen 17 is a beastly gaming laptop with a sleek, albeit heavy design. But if you want the most power in a form factor that you can take with you more easily, this is the best laptop HP makes.

HP Dragonfly G4
Best business laptop

One of the sleekest business laptops around

$1401 $2548 Save $1147

The HP Dragonfly G4 looks just like its predecessor but that's not a bad thing. It has a sleek design and a tall 3:2 display including an optional privacy screen, and it has a 5MP webcam. This model comes with upgraded processors that deliver even better performance than last year's.

$1401 at HP $1655 at B&H $1409 at Walmart

Business laptops are one of HP's strong suits, and the Dragonfly G4 is the latest entry in the company's most premium family, which was called Elite Dragonfly until last year. Not much has changed for this year's model, but it didn't need to, considering its predecessor was already great.

The major change for this year is on the inside, with new 13th-generation Intel Core processors, up to a Core i7-1365U with vPro. It comes with 10 cores and 12 threads, and it can boost up to 5.2GHz, so it's a noticeable upgrade from its predecessor, and it should have better battery life, too. You also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD, more than enough for the use case of this laptop.

The 13.5-inch display is essentially the same as last year, and it borrows from the Spectre x360 above. It has a tall 3:2 aspect ratio, which is still pretty uncommon, and it comes in Full HD+ resolution by default, with the option for HP Sure View Reflect if you want a privacy screen. There's also an OLED panel option with 3000x2000 resolution, which will give you the best viewing experience. Above that display sits a 5MP webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition, still one of the best in any laptop.

Similarly, the design is basically unchanged from its predecessor, but the Dragonfly G4 still looks great. It weighs just 2.2 pounds, and it's 16.26mm thick, and it looks pretty great in its Slate Blue colorway (a silver option should also be available at some point). It also has a good supply of ports for a modern premium laptop, including two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a nano-SIM card slot for the cellular-enabled models. On that note, you can configure it with either 4G LTE or 5G connectivity, both powered by Intel modems.

The most notable downside of the HP Dragonfly G4 is that it costs a pretty penny outside of sales. That's pretty standard for business laptops, but it's important to keep an eye out for sales with this one if you want to get a good deal.

HP Spectre x360 16 (2023)
Best 15/16-inch laptop

Large display for productivity and creative work

$1200 $1700 Save $500

The 2023 HP Spectre x360 16 comes with upgraded Intel processors and optional Intel Arc graphics for workloads that require more GPU power. It has a super-sharp 16-inch display, including an optional OLED panel, too.

Pros
  • Powerful 13th-gen Intel processors and optional Intel Arc graphics
  • Very sharp 16-inch display, plus an OLED option
  • Beautiful dual-tone design
  • 5MP webcam
Cons
  • A little pricy
  • Somewhat heavy
$1200 at HP $1650 at Best Buy

If you want an HP laptop with a larger screen, you can't get much better than the HP Spectre x360 16. It's a powerful laptop with a versatile and striking design.

Speaking of power, the HP Spectre x360 16 comes with new 13th-generation Intel Core processors, and you can choose between a Core i7-13700H and a Core i7-1360P. If you choose the former, you get a more powerful CPU but no discrete graphics, while the latter option is paired with an Intel Arc A370M GPU if you need that kind of power. While the CPUs have been upgraded, the GPU is the same as last year's model. You can also configure the laptop with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

The 16-inch panel comes in a 16:10 aspect ratio, and it's great in every configuration. The base model comes in a super sharp 3092x1920 resolution, but you can go even sharper with the 4K Ultra HD+ OLED model, which also has more vibrant colors and true blacks, typical of OLED. Those are the same options available last year, and the 5MP webcam is the same too, but it's still great, offering Windows Hello support, too.

In terms of design, the HP Spectre x360 16 hasn't changed much, and it's available in Nightfall Black or Nocturne Blue colors, sporting a dual-tone look similar to the 13.5-inch model. Due to the larger size, it's obviously heavier, coming in at 4.74 pounds, which makes this a little less portable. For ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, HDMI, and a headphone jack, so there's solid connectivity overall.

All in all, this is an absolutely stellar laptop, and while it is a bit pricey, you definitely get your money's worth with it. It's not just one of HP's best laptops, it's one of the best laptops in general.

HP Envy 17 (2023)
Best 17-inch laptop

For those who want really large screens

$650 $1050 Save $400

The HP Envy 17 is a premium 17-inch laptop with an all-aluminum build and high-end specs, including 13th-generation Intel Core processors and optional Nvidia GeForce graphics. The large screen is ideal for multitasking, especially if you opt for the super-sharp 4K panel.

Pros
  • Large 17-inch display is great for productivity
  • Fast 13th-generation Intel CPUs and optional Nvidia graphics
  • 5MP webcam with auto frame and Windows Hello
Cons
  • You can only get Nvidia graphics with a 15W CPU
  • Pretty heavy
$650 at HP $1350 at Best Buy $849 at B&H

Need something a little bigger? Look no further than the fantastic HP Envy 17, a laptop with a large 17-inch display and enough power for all your day-to-day tasks, wrapped in a premium aluminum build.

The HP Envy 17 can be had with either an Intel Core i7-13700H or a Core i7-1355U processor. The former is faster but uses more power, while the latter is more power-efficient, but the U-series model comes with an Nvidia GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU, giving you a big boost in performance for things like video editing and gaming. The GPU isn't the fastest around, but it should be able to run a lot of games if you don't mind tweaking some settings. You can also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD.

The display is a 17.3-inch panel, and the base configuration comes in Full HD resolution and a 16:9 aspect ratio. You can upgrade by adding touch support or upgrade to a 4K display, which makes a lot of sense for this large display. Above the display, we're looking at a 5MP webcam with all the smart features we've mentioned before, plus Windows Hello support.

Being this is a laptop with a large screen and a solid aluminum build, it does add up to a fairly heavy machine, weighing around 5.5 pounds. However, you do get a great selection of ports, including two Thunderbolt 4 connections, three USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. The large size also makes room for a number pad on the side. A potential downside of the Envy 17 is that it only comes in silver, so it's not a very unique-looking machine.

If you want a large screen and a premium experience, the HP Envy 17 is the best option in HP's lineup and one of the best in general.

HP Envy 16 (2023)
Best laptop for creators

Powerful performance in a sleek design

$1199 $12991510 Save $12990311

The HP Envy 16 comes with up to a powerful Intel Core i9 processor and Nvidia GeForce RTX 4060 graphics, making it ideal for demanding creator workloads and even some gaming, while keeping a sleek design. It even includes a stunning OLED display with a dynamic refresh rate up to 120Hz.

Pros
  • Powerful Intel CPUs and Nvidia RTX 40 series graphics
  • 16:10 display with stunning OLED 120Hz option
  • Premium and clean design
Cons
  • A bit on the heavy side
  • Looks may be a bit boring
$1550 at HP $1750 at Best Buy $1199 at B&H

Content creators need a lot of power, but gaming laptops can be heavy and garish. If you want that balance of power and style, the HP Envy 16 is great for you.

For starters, the laptop is powered by up to an Intel Core i9-13900H processor, meaning you get up to 14 cores and 20 threads, along with speeds up to 5.4GHz. For graphics, you get up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU, which gives you plenty of power for video editing and gaming. Additionally, you can configure the laptop with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD for storage.

Display-wise, the base model comes with a Quad HD+ panel with a 120Hz refresh rate, which can be upgraded with touch support if you have use for it. However, even more interesting is the 2.8K OLED touch-enabled option, which is even sharper and has a dynamic refresh rate that can adjust between 48Hz and 120Hz. Above that display, there's a 5MP webcam with smart features and Windows Hello support, a standard for HP's premium laptops since 2022.

HP's design language doesn't change often, and this is another silver laptop made from aluminum, so it looks and feels premium, but it's arguably too subdued. Because of the premium build quality and powerful specs, it's a somewhat heavy machine at 5.17 pounds. On the other hand, the ports are very good. With two Thunderbolt 4 and two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader, you have plenty of options for connecting peripherals to your laptop. You can bypass docking stations with all the built-in ports, but having Thunderbolt does give you expansion options if you need them.

For what it offers, the HP Envy 16 isn't overly expensive, and it's an easy recommendation.

HP Pavilion Aero 13 (2023)
Best affordable lightweight laptop

Lightweight and capable at a reasonable price

$580 $880 Save $300

If you want a lightweight laptop that isn't overly expensive, the HP Pavilion Aero 13 is a fantastic choice. With AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors and a 16:10 display, you're getting great performance and multitasking capabilities.

Pros
  • Powerful AMD Ryzen 7035 series processors
  • Tall 16:10 display with optional Quad HD+ resolution
  • It weighs under 2.2 pounds and comes in four colors
Cons
  • 720p webcam
  • Newer models have been rising in price
$580 at HP $930 at HP (customizable) $800 at Amazon

If the laptops above are a little too expensive for you, the HP Pavilion Aero 13 is a fantastic option that's more affordable while still being very capable and surprisingly lightweight.

The latest model comes with powerful AMD Ryzen 7035 processors, up to a Ryzen 7 7735U with 8 cores and 16 threads. It offers really strong performance, and it's a major upgrade from previous models, especially in terms of integrated graphics, which are good enough for light gaming now. Combined with up to 16GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD, it makes for a great experience.

HP hasn't made many changes to the display, so we're looking at a 13.3-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio. The base model comes in Full HD+ resolution, but you can upgrade to a Quad HD+ panel, which makes this an excellent choice at this price point. The downside here is the webcam, which is still a 720p sensor without facial recognition, a clear step down from HP's more premium laptops. There's a fingerprint reader for Windows Hello, at least.

Another great thing about this laptop is the design. For starters, it weighs just under 2.2 pounds, which is super light for such a cheap laptop, and that's on top of coming in four colors to choose from. It has a fairly basic design, but these color options make help it stand out. And for ports, you get one USB Type-C and two Type-A ports, HDMI, and a headphone jack, which is a solid setup.

HP has been increasing the price of the Pavilion Aero over the past couple of years, but this is still a great and relatively affordable laptop. Sales have been frequent with past models, so it's worth keeping an eye out if you're interested in saving more money. We reviewed the original model back in 2021 if you'd like to know more.

HP Dragonfly Pro Chromebook
Best Chromebook

Powerful processors and a stunning display

HP makes some of the best Chromebooks around, and the Dragonfly Pro Chromebook is no exception. With top-tier Intel processors, a bright display, and even some RGB lighting, this is an exceptional machine if you want a Chromebook. Unfortunately, it's pricier than most Chrome OS laptops.

Pros
  • 12th-generation Intel CPUs delivering fantastic performance on Chrome OS
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display with a whopping 1,200 nits of brightness
  • 8MP webcam
Cons
  • A little heavier than it could be
  • Very expensive for a Chromebook
$1000 at HP

HP has been going strong with its Chromebooks, and the latest Dragonfly Pro Chromebook, despite the odd naming convention, is a great example of why. It's a fantastic machine in almost every way, with only a couple of notable downsides.

For starters, performance is handled by a 12th-generation Intel Core i5-1235U, a processor with 10 cores and 12 threads that can boost up to 4.4GHz. It's not the cream of the crop anymore, but it's far from a slow processor, and more importantly, it's perfect for ChromeOS, which is a much lighter operating system than Windows. The only configuration available also includes 16GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD, rounding out a great experience.

One of the highlights of this laptop is the display, which is a 14-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio and super-sharp Quad HD+ resolution. That's already great, but what's even more special is that this display can reach an impressive 1,200 nits of brightness, making it easily visible just about anywhere. Likewise, the webcam is one of the best you'll see on any laptop thanks to the 8MP sensor. ChromeOS doesn't support facial recognition for login, but the laptop has a fingerprint reader instead.

As far as the design goes, the HP Dragonfly Pro Chromebook looks great in both Ceramic White and Sparkling Black, and it even has a cool RGB-backlit keyboard to give it a bit of extra flair. One downside is that it's a little heavy considering the size, coming in at 3.33 pounds. It's not super heavy, but not as portable as it could be. It also doesn't have a ton of ports, though it does include a whopping four Thunderbolt 4 connections. However, it lacks things like a headphone jack, so you may need some adapters.

Despite some design blunders, the HP Dragonfly Pro Chromebook is a fantastic laptop. Aside from being a little heavy, the biggest downside is that it's expensive, coming in at $1,000. However, it's easily the best ChromeOS experience the company offers.

HP Chromebook Plus 15a
Best affordable Chromebook

Solid performance and premium design at a great price

$349 $499 Save $150

Despite the affordable price tag, the HP Chromebook Plus 15a is a very capable ChromeOS laptop, featuring an 8-core processor, along with 8GB of RAM and 128GB of UFS storage. It also has a large Full HD display and a premium design using recycled aluminum.

Pros
  • 8-core Intel processor delivers solid performance for everyday use
  • Large Full HD display isn't always a guarantee at this price
  • Sleek design with recycled aluminum for a premium feel
Cons
  • Not the lightest laptop around
$349 at Best Buy $430 at HP

HP makes a ton of Chromebooks, and if the Dragonfly Pro Chromebook is too expensive for you, the recently-launched HP Chromebook Plus 15a is an excellent alternative.

Inside this laptop is an Intel Core i3-N305 processor, which is a low-power CPU with eight cores and eight threads. That's more than enough for solid performance in day-to-day tasks, and it comes, especially with a lightweight platform like ChromeOS. Plus, it has 8GB of RAM and 128GB of UFS storage, so it has great performance for the price.

The display is solid, too. At this price range, it's not always guaranteed you'll get a Full HD panel, especially on a 15-inch laptop, but that's exactly what you get here, which is great to see. What's even rarer is a 1080p webcam, but that's included, too, so it's really hard to complain about what you're getting at this price.

Coming around to the design, the laptop features a sleek silver look with a chassis that uses recycled aluminum and plastic for a laptop that still feels premium despite its low price. It weighs 3.81 pounds, which is reasonable for a 15-inch laptop. As for ports, it has two USB-C and one USB Type-A, plus a headphone jack and a microSD card reader.

For $500, this is a very good laptop, but if you can find it on sale (as it is at writing time), then it's an absolute steal.

Best HP laptops in 2023: Bottom line

These are the best laptops you can get from HP right now. Whether it's a gift for a loved one or for yourself, these are all fantastic options for different types of users. For most people, the HP Spectre x360 13.5 is likely the best because it's got it all: Great performance, a stunning display that's even better with the OLED model, and a great webcam, all packed in a beautiful premium design that's not overly heavy.

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best overall
$900 $1500 Save $600

The 2023 HP Spectre x360 13.5 packs 13th-generation Intel Core processors in a sleek and stylish design. It also has a great 13.5-inch display with a 3:2 aspect ratio that makes it optimal for productivity.

$900 at Best Buy

But of course, there's something for everyone, and if your budget is a little tighter, it's always worth looking at the Pavilion Aero, which offers absolutely stellar value for its price. It is a bit more expensive than its predecessors, but this year's model is also significantly more powerful thanks to AMD's Ryzen 7035 series processor, while still having a great display and a very lightweight design. If you want to save even more and you don't need anything fancy, you can go with the HP Chromebook x360 13.3, too.

We included a couple of cellular-connected laptops on this list, but if you're looking for more, check out our list of the best 5G laptops you can buy today. There are plenty of great options there too.
item.description:
Few brands in the laptop world are as renowned and popular as HP. From budget-friendly laptops to high-end premium experiences, the company offers some of the best laptops you can buy. If you're looking to upgrade your current personal or work computer, or you want to gift someone else, going with HP is definitely a smart move. But there are plenty of options to choose from in the company's lineup and choosing the right one for you may prove difficult. To help you with that, we've rounded up the best HP laptops you can buy today.

HP covers a lot of ground, and whether you want a Windows 11 laptop or a Chromebook, there's something for you. We've chosen models geared at all kinds of different users, so whatever you're looking for, we've got you covered.

  • HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
    Best overall
    $900 at Best Buy
  • Omen 17 (Intel, 2023)
    Best gaming laptop
    $1556 at Amazon
  • HP Dragonfly G4
    Best business laptop
    $1401 at HP
  • HP Spectre x360 16 (2023)
    Best 15/16-inch laptop
    $1200 at HP
  • HP Envy 17 (2023)
    Best 17-inch laptop
    $650 at HP
  • HP Envy 16 (2023)
    Best laptop for creators
    $1199 at B&H
  • HP Pavilion Aero 13 (2023)
    Best affordable lightweight laptop
    $580 at HP
  • HP Dragonfly Pro Chromebook
    Best Chromebook
    $1000 at HP
  • HP Chromebook Plus 15a
    Best affordable Chromebook
    $349 at Best Buy

Our top picks for HP laptops in 2023

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best overall

A premium laptop with very few flaws

$900 $1500 Save $600

The 2023 HP Spectre x360 13.5 packs 13th-generation Intel Core processors in a sleek and stylish design. It also has a great 13.5-inch display with a 3:2 aspect ratio that makes it optimal for productivity.

Pros
  • Great performance and battery life with 13th-gen Intel processors
  • Premium all-aluminum design with a dual-tone look
  • Tall 3:2 display is great for working on the go
  • 5MP webcam
Cons
  • 5G support not yet available for this year's model
$950 at HP $900 at Best Buy $1350 at Best Buy (OLED)

The HP Spectre x360 is one of the most iconic Windows laptops out there, and with the new Spectre x360 13.5, it's just as easy to recommend as previous models.

Performanmce-wise, the HP Spectre x360 13.5 is packing up to a 13th-generation Intel Core i7-1355U, with 10 cores and 12 threads delivering strong performance and great battery life for a Windows laptop. You also get 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD for storage, though more configurations are likely to show up over time.

The 13.5-inch display on the HP Spectre x360 is also great, featuring a 3:2 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution in the base model. You have the option to get a 3K2K OLED panel, too, which is even sharper and produces extremely vibrant colors and pure blacks, making for a fantastic viewing experience on top of being a great canvas for work. Above the display is a 5MP webcam, which continues to be one of the best you can find on any laptop, and it also supports Windows Hello. It has some smart features, too, like auto framing and lighting correction.

HP hasn't really changed the design for this year, so we get the typical dual-tone look in both Nightfall Black and Nocturne Blue colorways.It looks great, while still being somewhat subdued. Being an all-aluminum laptop, the SPectre x360 13.5 weighs in at 3.01 pounds. A single-tone Natural Silver model should also be available at some point. As for ports, we're looking at two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader, a solid setup for a laptop of this size.

All in all, this is a fantastic premium convertible that gives you a tall high-quality screen, a striking premium design, and great performance all around. It's definitely one of the best laptops HP makes.

Omen 17 (Intel, 2023)
Best gaming laptop

Powerful performance and a smooth display

$1556 $1700 Save $144

The Omen 17 is HP's most powerful gaming laptop, with 13th-generation Intel HX-series processors and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU delivering outstanding performance. You also get a great display for gaming and up to 32GB of RAM.

Pros
  • Top-tier performance with 13th-gen Intel CPUs and Nvidia RTX 40 series GPUs
  • Sleek design with RGB for some flair
  • Plenty of ports
Cons
  • Battery life is abysmal
  • Still using a 720p webcam without Windows Hello
$1700 at HP (Core i7) $2600 at HP (Core i9) $1556 at Amazon

HP also caters to gamers with its Omen lineup of laptops, and the Omen 17 is the cream of the crop if you want to get into gaming.

Performance is the name of the game here, and with up to an Intel Core i9-13900HX with 24 cores and 32 threads, the Omen 17 is off to a very good start. What's more, that's paired with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, giving you enough power to run just about any modern game with no issues. You also get up 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

The display is a 17.3-inch panel and it comes in the typical 16:9 aspect ratio, which is becoming less common. TYThe base model is Full HD and has a 144Hz refresh rate, but you can upgrade to a Quad HD panel and choose either 165Hz or 240Hz models depending on how smooth you want your games to be. Both options are very good, though. The same can't be said for the webcam, which is still a 720p camera with no Windows Hello support.

In terms of design, the Omen 17 looks fairly sleek and clean, and even though it does have RGB, it's done in a very subdued way so it's not obnoxious at all. It's still a heavy laptop, though, starting at 6.1 pounds, and that's to be expected with such powerful specs. You also get a healthy supply of ports. There are three USB Type-A ports (which many peripherals still use), a Thunderbolt 4/USB-C port, HDMI 2.1, mini DisplayPort, Gigabit Ethernet, microSD card reader, and a headphone jack. Plus, of course, a proprietary power connector.

Overall, the Omen 17 is a beastly gaming laptop with a sleek, albeit heavy design. But if you want the most power in a form factor that you can take with you more easily, this is the best laptop HP makes.

HP Dragonfly G4
Best business laptop

One of the sleekest business laptops around

$1401 $2548 Save $1147

The HP Dragonfly G4 looks just like its predecessor but that's not a bad thing. It has a sleek design and a tall 3:2 display including an optional privacy screen, and it has a 5MP webcam. This model comes with upgraded processors that deliver even better performance than last year's.

$1401 at HP $1655 at B&H $1409 at Walmart

Business laptops are one of HP's strong suits, and the Dragonfly G4 is the latest entry in the company's most premium family, which was called Elite Dragonfly until last year. Not much has changed for this year's model, but it didn't need to, considering its predecessor was already great.

The major change for this year is on the inside, with new 13th-generation Intel Core processors, up to a Core i7-1365U with vPro. It comes with 10 cores and 12 threads, and it can boost up to 5.2GHz, so it's a noticeable upgrade from its predecessor, and it should have better battery life, too. You also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD, more than enough for the use case of this laptop.

The 13.5-inch display is essentially the same as last year, and it borrows from the Spectre x360 above. It has a tall 3:2 aspect ratio, which is still pretty uncommon, and it comes in Full HD+ resolution by default, with the option for HP Sure View Reflect if you want a privacy screen. There's also an OLED panel option with 3000x2000 resolution, which will give you the best viewing experience. Above that display sits a 5MP webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition, still one of the best in any laptop.

Similarly, the design is basically unchanged from its predecessor, but the Dragonfly G4 still looks great. It weighs just 2.2 pounds, and it's 16.26mm thick, and it looks pretty great in its Slate Blue colorway (a silver option should also be available at some point). It also has a good supply of ports for a modern premium laptop, including two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a nano-SIM card slot for the cellular-enabled models. On that note, you can configure it with either 4G LTE or 5G connectivity, both powered by Intel modems.

The most notable downside of the HP Dragonfly G4 is that it costs a pretty penny outside of sales. That's pretty standard for business laptops, but it's important to keep an eye out for sales with this one if you want to get a good deal.

HP Spectre x360 16 (2023)
Best 15/16-inch laptop

Large display for productivity and creative work

$1200 $1700 Save $500

The 2023 HP Spectre x360 16 comes with upgraded Intel processors and optional Intel Arc graphics for workloads that require more GPU power. It has a super-sharp 16-inch display, including an optional OLED panel, too.

Pros
  • Powerful 13th-gen Intel processors and optional Intel Arc graphics
  • Very sharp 16-inch display, plus an OLED option
  • Beautiful dual-tone design
  • 5MP webcam
Cons
  • A little pricy
  • Somewhat heavy
$1200 at HP $1650 at Best Buy

If you want an HP laptop with a larger screen, you can't get much better than the HP Spectre x360 16. It's a powerful laptop with a versatile and striking design.

Speaking of power, the HP Spectre x360 16 comes with new 13th-generation Intel Core processors, and you can choose between a Core i7-13700H and a Core i7-1360P. If you choose the former, you get a more powerful CPU but no discrete graphics, while the latter option is paired with an Intel Arc A370M GPU if you need that kind of power. While the CPUs have been upgraded, the GPU is the same as last year's model. You can also configure the laptop with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

The 16-inch panel comes in a 16:10 aspect ratio, and it's great in every configuration. The base model comes in a super sharp 3092x1920 resolution, but you can go even sharper with the 4K Ultra HD+ OLED model, which also has more vibrant colors and true blacks, typical of OLED. Those are the same options available last year, and the 5MP webcam is the same too, but it's still great, offering Windows Hello support, too.

In terms of design, the HP Spectre x360 16 hasn't changed much, and it's available in Nightfall Black or Nocturne Blue colors, sporting a dual-tone look similar to the 13.5-inch model. Due to the larger size, it's obviously heavier, coming in at 4.74 pounds, which makes this a little less portable. For ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, HDMI, and a headphone jack, so there's solid connectivity overall.

All in all, this is an absolutely stellar laptop, and while it is a bit pricey, you definitely get your money's worth with it. It's not just one of HP's best laptops, it's one of the best laptops in general.

HP Envy 17 (2023)
Best 17-inch laptop

For those who want really large screens

$650 $1050 Save $400

The HP Envy 17 is a premium 17-inch laptop with an all-aluminum build and high-end specs, including 13th-generation Intel Core processors and optional Nvidia GeForce graphics. The large screen is ideal for multitasking, especially if you opt for the super-sharp 4K panel.

Pros
  • Large 17-inch display is great for productivity
  • Fast 13th-generation Intel CPUs and optional Nvidia graphics
  • 5MP webcam with auto frame and Windows Hello
Cons
  • You can only get Nvidia graphics with a 15W CPU
  • Pretty heavy
$650 at HP $1350 at Best Buy $849 at B&H

Need something a little bigger? Look no further than the fantastic HP Envy 17, a laptop with a large 17-inch display and enough power for all your day-to-day tasks, wrapped in a premium aluminum build.

The HP Envy 17 can be had with either an Intel Core i7-13700H or a Core i7-1355U processor. The former is faster but uses more power, while the latter is more power-efficient, but the U-series model comes with an Nvidia GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU, giving you a big boost in performance for things like video editing and gaming. The GPU isn't the fastest around, but it should be able to run a lot of games if you don't mind tweaking some settings. You can also get up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD.

The display is a 17.3-inch panel, and the base configuration comes in Full HD resolution and a 16:9 aspect ratio. You can upgrade by adding touch support or upgrade to a 4K display, which makes a lot of sense for this large display. Above the display, we're looking at a 5MP webcam with all the smart features we've mentioned before, plus Windows Hello support.

Being this is a laptop with a large screen and a solid aluminum build, it does add up to a fairly heavy machine, weighing around 5.5 pounds. However, you do get a great selection of ports, including two Thunderbolt 4 connections, three USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader. The large size also makes room for a number pad on the side. A potential downside of the Envy 17 is that it only comes in silver, so it's not a very unique-looking machine.

If you want a large screen and a premium experience, the HP Envy 17 is the best option in HP's lineup and one of the best in general.

HP Envy 16 (2023)
Best laptop for creators

Powerful performance in a sleek design

$1199 $12991510 Save $12990311

The HP Envy 16 comes with up to a powerful Intel Core i9 processor and Nvidia GeForce RTX 4060 graphics, making it ideal for demanding creator workloads and even some gaming, while keeping a sleek design. It even includes a stunning OLED display with a dynamic refresh rate up to 120Hz.

Pros
  • Powerful Intel CPUs and Nvidia RTX 40 series graphics
  • 16:10 display with stunning OLED 120Hz option
  • Premium and clean design
Cons

  • Looks may be a bit boring
$1550 at HP $1750 at Best Buy $1199 at B&H

Lightweight and capable at a reasonable price

$580 $880 Save $300

$580 at HP $930 at HP (customizable) $800 at Amazon
